notes: Issue #42 follow-up, authored by Claude after the researcher confirmed the Thai monarch IS in scope. PURE-WAYBACK recipe recipes/tha_royaloffice_wayback.yml — the live royaloffice.th is hard-blocked by a Cloudflare interactive challenge (403 for bot UA, browser UA, AND a real headless Chromium given 9s to run the JS challenge), so no live recipe and no wayback_to bound. Recipe-only, engine untouched. Probe --spread: 1549 snapshots; title(<title>)/text(.entry-content, 404-871 chars) green; dates parsed 2017-10-01, 2021-06-04, 2022-04-29, 2022-10-31, 2023-07-26. STRUCTURE: WordPress dated permalinks /YYYY/MM/DD/<thai-slug>; start_urls uses ONE CDX PREFIX PER YEAR (2017-2026) because a bare royaloffice.th prefix returns ~6000 captures dominated by /court-circular/page/N listings and /document/ assets — pure-wayback passes every start_url to the CDX as its own prefix query. The /en/ mirror is deliberately NOT harvested: it is only partially translated (its pages still carry Thai titles and Thai bodies), so it would duplicate rows without adding English. TWO TRAPS HANDLED: (1) TITLE — <h1 class='title'> is the SECTION header and always reads 'News and Activities'; it is NOT the article title, which exists only in <title>. Do not 'fix' this to h1. (2) DATE — the page prints .single-date, but on the Thai side that renders in the THAI BUDDHIST ERA (BE = CE + 543): '2565' means 2022, and dateparser would read it as a literal Gregorian year, landing every row ~543 years in the future. Date therefore comes from url_regex ONLY (the /YYYY/MM/DD/ permalink is Gregorian). This is not hypothetical — a sampled slug literally contains '_22-04-2565' for a 2022-04-29 item. CONTENT CAVEAT before a full run: this is the Bureau of the Royal Household's announcement feed (court-circular style), written in the THIRD PERSON about royal activities ('His Majesty graciously permitted the organisation of ...'), NOT transcripts of the King's own words; bodies are short (404-871 chars). The cleaner's gate keeps official_statement incl. third-person communiqués conveying the leader's position, so expect these to land as official_statement with FEW true speeches (Thai royal speech transcripts are scarce online generally). speaker_default omitted; archive spans Rama X (Vajiralongkorn, acceded 2016). Coverage by URL date: 2017:1, 2018:8, 2019:27, 2020:163, 2021:374, 2022:495, 2023:402, 2024:135. country=Thailand; source_url=https://www.royaloffice.th/ (via Internet Archive); source_name=royaloffice.th; source_type=official_gov; region=Asia; iso3n=764; content_format=fulltext; leaders_covered=monarch. FULL RUN pending (researcher gate).
